result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
I have already covered foundational benchmarks for distribution shifts (WILDS), environmental corruption robustness (ImageNet-C), and the theoretical mechanism of shortcut learning. A critical missing piece for real-world deployment pitfalls is **model calibration and uncertainty estimation**. Deep learning models are notoriously overconfident on out-of-distribution or shifted data, which leads to high-stakes failures in practice when their predictions are trusted blindly. Citing Guo et al. (2017) will allow me to discuss the gap between accuracy and reliability, introduce established calibration methods (like temperature scaling), compare findings on modern architectures' miscalibration, and support the argument that uncertainty quantification must be integrated into pre-deployment validation pipelines. This effectively addresses the "Using Specific Concepts", "Comparing Findings", "Highlighting Research Gaps", and "Supporting Arguments" categories while rounding out the refe
